title: monitoring — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. MonitoringAlertFired (P1 severity) + MonitoringIncidentOpened + MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened are the CRITICAL events of the module — every fire pages on-call. MonitoringIncidentEscalated is the escalation signal (unacknowledged past SLA). MonitoringHealthCheckPersistentlyFailingNotification signals a persistently unhealthy check that has not yet crossed an alert threshold — a leading indicator.

events.name: HealthCheckCreated
events.trigger: HealthCheckObserver.created committed a new health_checks row.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — infrastructure config is compliance-relevant)
monitoring::ScheduleFirstRun (dispatches RunHealthCheckJob delayed by interval_seconds/2 so the check doesn't fire at boot burst)

events.name: HealthCheckUpdated
events.trigger: HealthCheckObserver.updated committed changes to a health_checks row. Interval or target changes require re-scheduling.
consumers: monitoring::MaybeRescheduleHealthCheck (reschedules RunHealthCheckJob when interval_seconds or target changed)

events.name: HealthCheckEnabled
events.trigger: HealthCheckObserver.updated detected is_enabled transition false → true.

events.name: HealthCheckDisabled
events.trigger: HealthCheckObserver.updated detected is_enabled transition true → false.
consumers: monitoring::CancelPendingRuns (removes queued RunHealthCheckJob for this check)

events.name: HealthCheckRun
events.trigger: HealthCheckRunObserver.created inserted a new health_check_runs row. High-volume — one fire per (health_check × interval_seconds).
consumers: monitoring::EvaluateAlertPolicies (checks whether this run triggers any threshold breaches)
monitoring::UpdateHealthCheckDenormalizedState (updates last_run_at + last_run_status + consecutive_failure_count on the parent)

events.name: HealthCheckStatusChanged
events.trigger: HealthCheckRunObserver.created detected the new run's status differs from the parent's last_run_status. Signals a transition (healthy → degraded, unhealthy → healthy, …).
consumers: monitoring::EvaluateAlertPolicies (transition signals may trigger policies that watch state changes)
monitoring::MaybeFirePersistentFailureNotification (when new status is unhealthy AND consecutive_failure_count >= 7: fire MonitoringHealthCheckPersistentlyFailingNotification)

events.name: MonitoringAlertPolicyCreated
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertPolicyObserver.created committed a new monitoring_alert_policies row.
consumers: monitoring::PrimeEvaluatorCache (warms the AlertPolicyEvaluator's per-tenant policy cache)

events.name: MonitoringAlertPolicyUpdated
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertPolicyObserver.updated committed changes to a monitoring_alert_policies row.

events.name: MonitoringAlertPolicySuppressed
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertPolicyObserver.updated detected suppression_config.silenced_until was set OR after_hours_suppress / weekend_suppress toggled.
consumers: notifications::DispatchMonitoringPolicySuppressedNotification (audit trail — someone silenced a policy; other admins should know)

events.name: MonitoringAlertFired
events.trigger: CRITICAL. MonitoringAlertObserver.created inserted a new monitoring_alerts row with status='firing'. The AlertPolicyEvaluator detected a threshold breach that met the policy's consecutive_breaches_required. Every P1 fire pages on-call.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — every alert is a compliance-relevant safety signal)
monitoring::GroupIntoIncident (dispatches GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob to attach or open an incident)
monitoring::NotifyProviders (dispatches NotifyProvidersJob for the policy's notify_provider_configs)
notifications::DispatchMonitoringAlertFiredNotification (P1: page on-call, P2: alert primary, P3+P4: dashboard-only)

events.name: MonitoringAlertAcknowledged
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertObserver.updated detected status transition firing → acknowledged. A human took ownership.
consumers: monitoring::MaybeAckIncident (if this was the last unacknowledged alert in its incident → transition incident.status = 'acknowledged')

events.name: MonitoringAlertResolved
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertObserver.updated detected status transition to 'resolved'. Either signal cleared (auto-resolve) or ops manually resolved.
consumers: monitoring::MaybeResolveIncident (if this was the last firing alert in its incident → transition incident.status = 'resolved')

events.name: MonitoringAlertSuppressed
events.trigger: MonitoringAlertObserver.created inserted a row with status='suppressed'. The threshold breached but the policy's suppression_config matched (after-hours, weekend, silenced_until).
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (compliance record — 'we saw this signal but chose not to page')

events.name: MonitoringIncidentOpened
events.trigger: CRITICAL. MonitoringIncidentObserver.created inserted a new monitoring_incidents row with status='open'. GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob determined the fired alert did not attach to any existing incident + opened a new one.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — every incident is compliance-relevant)
notifications::DispatchMonitoringIncidentOpenedNotification (P1: page on-call via oncall_page_config; P2+: notify primary; P3+: dashboard-only)
monitoring::NotifyProviders (dispatches NotifyProvidersJob for the incident's oncall_page_config)
monitoring::ScheduleEscalation (dispatches EscalateIncidentJob delayed by the severity's SLA)

payload.title: string
payload.initial_alert_id: ULID (the alert that opened the incident)

events.name: MonitoringIncidentAcknowledged
events.trigger: MonitoringIncidentObserver.updated detected status transition open → acknowledged. A human took ownership.
consumers: monitoring::CancelEscalation (the ack means we don't escalate this one)

events.name: MonitoringIncidentStatusUpdated
events.trigger: MonitoringIncidentObserver.updated detected status transition to investigating / mitigating / postmortem. Not fired for open / acknowledged / resolved — those have dedicated events.

events.name: MonitoringIncidentResolved
events.trigger: MonitoringIncidentObserver.updated detected status transition to 'resolved'. Either every alert in the incident resolved (auto) or ops manually resolved.
consumers: monitoring::MaybeSchedulePostmortem (when severity=p1: fire MonitoringPostmortemRequiredNotification + transition status to 'postmortem' if not already)

events.name: MonitoringProviderConfigured
events.trigger: MonitoringProviderConfigObserver.created committed a new provider_configs row.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — provider connection is compliance-relevant)
monitoring::MaybeDispatchProviderTest (when test_mode=true: fires MonitoringProviderTestSent via a synthetic alert)

events.name: MonitoringProviderDeactivated
events.trigger: MonitoringProviderConfigObserver.updated detected is_active → false OR row soft-deleted.

events.name: MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened
events.trigger: CRITICAL. consecutive_failure_count on a provider_config reached failure_threshold. Sets circuit_breaker_open_until = now() + open_duration_seconds. Blocks all subsequent notifications until the timer elapses. Every fire is a P1 signal — the tenant's monitoring is telling them their monitoring is broken.
consumers: notifications::DispatchMonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification (cannot-opt-out — pages ops + notifies tenant owner + falls back to alternate paging provider)
monitoring::FailoverToAlternateProvider (if this was PagerDuty: fail over to Opsgenie for pending notifications; if Opsgenie: fail over to PagerDuty)

events.name: MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerClosed
events.trigger: Provider recovered — half-open probe succeeded OR ops manually reset via monitoring:reset-circuit-breaker command / POST endpoint.

events.name: MonitoringProviderTestSent
events.trigger: POST /providers/{provider}/test invoked OR test_mode=true on a new config. Dispatches a synthetic alert via the provider's sandbox / test endpoint.
